title: functracer: Introductory vignette
code: library(functracer)  result <- analyze_dependencies(   entry_script = "path/to/main.R",   package_dir = "path/to/package-root" )  result  output <- trace_functions(   entry_script = "path/to/main.R",   package_dir = "path/to/package-root",   output_format = "svg",   output_dir = ".",   output_prefix = "analysis" )  output$output_path

title: functracer: Introductory vignette
text: example creates minimal package script temp folder.
code: library(functracer)  work_dir <- tempfile("functracer-demo-") dir.create(work_dir)  demo_pkg <- file.path(work_dir, "demoPkg") dir.create(demo_pkg) dir.create(file.path(demo_pkg, "R"))  writeLines(   c(     "Package: demoPkg",     "Version: 0.0.1",     "Title: Demo Package",     "Description: Minimal package for functracer vignette.",     "License: MIT"   ),   file.path(demo_pkg, "DESCRIPTION") )  writeLines(   c("export(core_fn)", "export(helper_fn)"),   file.path(demo_pkg, "NAMESPACE") )  writeLines(   c(     "core_fn <- function(x) {",     "  helper_fn(x)",     "}",     "",     "helper_fn <- function(x) {",     "  x + 1",     "}"   ),   file.path(demo_pkg, "R", "functions.R") )  entry_script <- file.path(work_dir, "main.R") writeLines(   c(     "run <- function(v) {",     "  core_fn(v)",     "}",     "",     "run(3)"   ),   entry_script )  result <- analyze_dependencies(   entry_script = entry_script,   package_dir = demo_pkg )  result #>    function dep_type hop_depth                             call_path  source #> 1   core_fn   direct         1                     main.R -> core_fn demoPkg #> 2       run   direct         1                         main.R -> run  main.R #> 3   core_fn indirect         2              main.R -> run -> core_fn demoPkg #> 4 helper_fn indirect         2        main.R -> core_fn -> helper_fn demoPkg #> 5 helper_fn indirect         3 main.R -> run -> core_fn -> helper_fn demoPkg #>   is_exported #> 1        TRUE #> 2       FALSE #> 3        TRUE #> 4        TRUE #> 5        TRUE

title: functracer: Introductory vignette
code: output <- trace_functions(   entry_script = entry_script,   package_dir = demo_pkg,   output_format = "csv",   output_dir = work_dir,   output_prefix = "demo" ) #> Dependency analysis complete #> Format: csv #> Output: /tmp/Rtmp2HdGw9/functracer-demo-1ae623934212/demo_dependencies.csv  out <- read.csv(output$output_path, check.names = FALSE) out #>    function dep_type hop_depth                             call_path  source #> 1   core_fn   direct         1                     main.R -> core_fn demoPkg #> 2       run   direct         1                         main.R -> run  main.R #> 3   core_fn indirect         2              main.R -> run -> core_fn demoPkg #> 4 helper_fn indirect         2        main.R -> core_fn -> helper_fn demoPkg #> 5 helper_fn indirect         3 main.R -> run -> core_fn -> helper_fn demoPkg #>   is_exported #> 1        TRUE #> 2       FALSE #> 3        TRUE #> 4        TRUE #> 5        TRUE

title: functracer: Introductory vignette
text: dep_type = direct means called entry script. dep_type = indirect means reached functions. Larger hop_depth means farther entry script. call_path gives exact chain used reach function. enough answer: “change function X, workflows affected?”

what: Trace Function Dependencies in R Code
title: Trace Function Dependencies in R Code
text: Trace direct transitive function dependencies R entry script target package source tree.

title: Trace Function Dependencies in R Code
text: Extract direct function calls entry script. Build package function adjacency R/ source files. Traverse transitive dependencies retain call paths. Export dependency artifacts CSV, JSON, SVG. Supports regular function assignments S7 generics/methods.

title: Trace Function Dependencies in R Code
code: # from the repository root install.packages(".", repos = NULL, type = "source")

title: Trace Function Dependencies in R Code
code: library(functracer)  result <- analyze_dependencies(   entry_script = "path/to/main.R",   package_dir = "path/to/package-root" )  result  output <- trace_functions(   entry_script = "path/to/main.R",   package_dir = "path/to/package-root",   output_format = "json",   output_dir = ".",   output_prefix = "analysis" )  output$output_path

title: Trace Function Dependencies in R Code
code: Rscript inst/scripts/functracer.R \   --entry path/to/main.R \   --package-dir path/to/package-root \   --output-format json \   --output-dir . \   --prefix analysis

title: Trace Function Dependencies in R Code
text: CSV includes: function dep_type (direct indirect) hop_depth call_path source is_exported

what: Plan: R Transitive Dependency Analysis for MOSuite Scripts
title: NA
text: TL;DR: Use treesitter (modern AST parser) extract function calls scripts, recursively trace MOSuite source code build complete dependency graph, output three formats (data frame impact analysis, JSON programmatic use, SVG graph visualization).

previous_headings: Plan: R Transitive Dependency Analysis for MOSuite Scripts
title: NA
text: Phase 1: Create function Extract Direct Function Calls script (depends treesitter availability) - Parse script (example: main.R) using treesitter::tree_parse() + walk AST find function calls - Optional: Filter namespace calls specific package (example: MOSuite), extract function name + line number - Output: data frame direct calls script Phase 2: Build Function Dependency Map given R package (parallel Phase 1) - Scan R files package (example: MOSuite/R/) extract function definitions - function, parse body record functions calls - Output: adjacency structure mapping function → dependencies Phase 3: Transitive Closure (depends Phases 1 & 2) - Start direct calls; recursively add dependencies - Track depth/hop count call path transitive dependency - Continue reaching leaf functions (dependencies) - Output: full transitive list metadata Phase 4: Multi-Format Output (depends Phase 3) - CSV/TSV: Sortable depth/type; columns: function, dep_type (direct/indirect), hop_depth, call_path - JSON: Nested depth programmatic queries - SVG graph: Nodes = functions, edges = dependencies, colored depth visual impact analysis

previous_headings: Plan: R Transitive Dependency Analysis for MOSuite Scripts
title: NA
text: MOSuite-plot-venn-diagram/code/main.R — example target script R — MOSuite source code example package NAMESPACE — MOSuite public API

previous_headings: Plan: R Transitive Dependency Analysis for MOSuite Scripts
title: NA
text: Phase 1: Manually confirm MOSuite calls main.R captured (e.g., load_moo_from_data_dir(), plot_volcano_summary(), plot_venn_diagram()) Phase 2: Spot-check one function (e.g., plot_volcano_summary) — verify dependencies match manual inspection source Phase 3: Verify transitive closure finds non-obvious dependencies (functions called indirectly) Output validation: duplicates, fields populated, answers “change function X, ’s affected?”

title: Trace Functions and Write One Output Format — trace_functions
code: trace_functions(   entry_script,   package_dir,   package_name = NULL,   output_dir = ".",   output_prefix = NULL,   output_format = c("csv", "json", "svg") )

title: Trace Functions and Write One Output Format — trace_functions
text: entry_script Path entry R script. package_dir Path package root directory containing R/ NAMESPACE. package_name Optional package name. NULL, inferred DESCRIPTION. output_dir Directory output artifacts written. output_prefix Prefix output files. Defaults entry script stem. output_format Output file format. One "csv", "json", "svg".

title: Trace Functions and Write One Output Format — trace_functions
text: Invisibly returns list output path dependency data.
